differentials.name: infiltrating ductal carcinoma
differentials.raw: infiltrating ductal carcinoma (75% of all invasive cancers)

differentials.name: intracystic carcinoma (rare): subareolar area; bloody aspiration
differentials.raw: intracystic carcinoma (rare): subareolar area; bloody aspiration

differentials.name: clinical size of mass > radiographic size
differentials.raw: clinical size of mass > radiographic size (1951) (Leborgne's law) [Raul Alfredo Leborgne Fossemale (1907-1986), Uruguayan radiologist created the mammograph, discovered radiological signs and developed the first method of radiologically guided mammary biopsy]

differentials.name: Microcalcifications associated with malignant mass by mammogram in 40%, pathologically with special stains in 60%, on specimen radiography in 86% ◊  20% of clustered microcalcifications represent a malignant process!
differentials.raw: Microcalcifications associated with malignant mass by mammogram in 40%, pathologically with special stains in 60%, on specimen radiography in 86% ◊  20% of clustered microcalcifications represent a malignant process!

context: =  PRIMARY SIGNS OF BREAST CANCER √  mass feels larger than its mammographic / sonographic size DDx:   prior biopsy / trauma / infection (b)  Smooth border √  "telltale" signs: lobulation, small comet tail, flattening of one side of the lesion, slight irregularity √  "halo" sign (= Mach band) may be present DDx:   cyst (sonographic evaluation) (c)  Lobulation appearance similar to fibroadenoma (only characteristic calcifications may exclude malignancy) ◊  The likelihood of malignancy increases with number of lobulations √  distinct central tumor mass with volumetric rather than planar appearan
